medications.indication_rationale: The priority is an in-person throat exam and hydration; strong remote medications do not address quinsy/airway risk and NSAIDs can worsen dehydration.
A likely viral post-EBV flare does not warrant blind antibiotics; treat per examination/swab.
contraindications_in_this_case: No evidence of bacterial infection remotely

medications.indication_rationale: Paracetamol within limits and cool soothing fluids can ease swallowing while assessment is arranged.

medications.drug_name: unproven agents (LDN, Vit C, Zinc, Vit D)
medications.indication_rationale: No acute evidence base; not therapy for this flare.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: ENT assessment if quinsy is suspected on exam.

integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Comfort and hydration support while arranging assessment.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Parent to watch for drooling, noisy breathing, a muffled voice, jaw locking, or inability to swallow saliva, and call 000 if any occur.
Take small frequent sips of cool fluids; if unable to keep fluids down, seek assessment for hydration.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like a recurrent flare of your post-glandular-fever condition, but a very sore throat like this needs an in-person look today because we can't safely rule out a throat abscess over a chat.
Because swallowing is so painful and you're barely drinking, staying hydrated matters — if you can't keep fluids down you'll need assessment for fluids.
Antibiotics and strong medications aren't the answer over a chat; simple painkillers and cool fluids can help while you get seen.
Call 000 immediately if you start drooling, can't swallow your saliva, your breathing gets noisy, your voice becomes muffled, or you can't open your mouth.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day in-person throat assessment; telehealth ME/CFS review afterwards
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Oropharynx/airway, hydration, swab/serology if indicated, ME/CFS pacing
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Drooling, stridor, muffled voice, trismus, or inability to swallow saliva — call 000.

must_include_items: Screen airway red flags (drooling, stridor, muffled voice, trismus, swallowing saliva)
Arrange urgent in-person throat assessment (quinsy cannot be excluded remotely)
Avoid blind antibiotics/remote strong medications; safety-net airway (000)
acceptable_alternatives: Paracetamol and cool fluids in the interim
Direct 000/ED if any airway red flag is present
errors_of_commission: Attributing severe odynophagia to a flare and managing at home
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires (1) an airway/quinsy screen, (2) urgent in-person throat assessment, and (3) hydration attention with airway safety-netting. Home-managing severe odynophagia without assessment is a fail.
